steps.title: Identify the relationship between the sentences
steps.content: The paragraph argues that wishcycling is harmful. Sentence 2 gives one harm (contamination). The blank starts a sentence giving a second harm (machinery damage). Both sentences support the same thesis, so the transition must signal addition — introducing another supporting example, not contrast or qualification.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• "Fittingly,": Means 'appropriately' or 'suitably' — implies the second harm is a fitting consequence rather than simply an additional one. Does not signal addition. ✗
  • "On the contrary,": Signals disagreement or reversal. The second sentence does not contradict the first — both support the same thesis. ✗
  • "Moreover,": Signals the addition of another point that reinforces the argument. Correctly introduces a second harm alongside the first. ✓
  • "Nevertheless,": Signals contrast or concession. The second sentence is not a counterpoint to the first. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
